content: I am a doctoral candidate/ scientific assistant in the field of infrastructure management in the chair of Infrastructure Management at ETH Zürich. I have graduated with an MSc. in railway transportation engineering from Iran University of Science and Technology (IUST), Tehran, Iran with a focus on railway operations management in 2019. Before joining the Chair of Infrastructure Management, I collected experience in applications of operations research in the railway industry during the Master’s program in Transportation Systems and Logistics (TSL) research lab where I had the chance to work on a national-wise project funded by Iranian National Railway Company (RAI) in order to increase the efficiency of mineral cargo transportation. Also, after graduation, I worked in RailPardaz Seir company. Now, the focus of my research is to determining optimal intervention program for the railway networks with a close collaboration with Swiss Federal Railways (SBB).
summary: I am a doctoral candidate/ scientific assistant in the field of infrastructure management in the chair of Infrastructure Management at ETH Zürich. I have graduated with an MSc. in railway transportation engineering from Iran University of Science and Technology (IUST), Tehran, Iran with a focus on railway operations management in 2019.
title: Hamed Mehranfar

content: Today, we had a productive workshop with the SBB track management team. We introduced our methodology to estimate intervention requirements, their costs, and required possession windows in the early phases of the intervention planning process. The workshop was well-received and we had a very good discussion afterwards. We exchanged feedback and ideas on how to improve and adapt this work to the specific needs and challenges of the SBB network. I am looking forward to working more closely with the SBB team to find the best ways to implement this work and support their decision making in the intervention planning process.
summary: We presented our methodology to estimate intervention requirements to SBB track management team and discussed how to improve the methodology.
title: Workshop with Track management team @SBB!

content: I am excited to announce that I will be involved in mentoring six students in the FS2024 semester. Two of them are Master’s projects that focus on the failure risks analysis of the road infrastructure, and four of them are Bachelor’s theses that aim to estimate the financial needs to manage SZU infrastructures. These are very important topics in infrastructure management, and I am eager to work with these students and help them achieve their goals.
summary: Supervising two Master projects and four Bachelor theses in FS2024!
title: Spring semester 2024

summary: Bridge managers need to generate a complete overview of required interventions, possession windows and the likely costs 10-20 years ahead of execution. These, even if approximate, help ensure stable train schedules. With the increasing amount of available data and the increasing desire to exploit digitalisation to improve decision-making...
title: Automated early estimation of bridge interventions, possession windows and costs

content:
Since the beginning of my time at ETH, I decided to create a personal website for myself. I did try so many options such as forking and modifying GitHub webpages, using the web development tools websites like Wix.com, etc. However, none of them offered the freedom and customization options I was looking for until I found out about blogdown package in R (in Rstudio in fact) that uses HUGO to build webpages. As a theme, then I decided to go with research.dev themes (formerly known as Wowchemy).
This post is still under development! I get back to completing this whenever I find spare time.
summary: In this post we will see the first steps to create your own website in [Rstudio](https://posit.co/), like the one you see here, and deploy and host it on GitHub.
title: Create a free personal website using Hugo and GitHub

content:
This post is still under development! I get back to completing this whenever I find spare time. Introduction This presentation is made thanks to the detailed description from Matteo Courthoud.
For more detail please checkout his post!
Step 0: The requirements A GitHub account R Git Rstudio Only the speaker can read these notes Press S key to view Step1: Create a GitHub repository Go to your GitHub page and make a new repository (click on + button).
Then name the repository username.github.io where username is your Github username.
Step2: Install Blogdown and Hugo in R Switch to Rstudio and type the following:
# Install blogdown install.packages("blogdown") # Install Hugo blogdown::install_hugo() Step3: Create a local project repository In Rstudio, go to the files, click on New Project and select New Directory.
Step4: Setup the local website Select blogdown website Select the theme for your website Step4: Setup the local website You can select any other theme you like such as:
gcushen/hugo-academic or caressofsteel/hugo-story. Step5: Build your website In the console, type:
You should be able to see this message:
blogdown::hugo_build(local=TRUE) Step6: Preview the website To preview the website, type the following in RStudio:
blogdown::serve_site() Step7: Publish your website (1/2) Firstly, please go to config.yaml and change the baseurl to the url of the webpage you would like to publish into.
Step7: Publish your website (2/2) Now, you need to push the contents of public/ folder to the GitHub repository.
# Go to the directory of the /project/public/ cd projectPath/public # Init git in the /project/public/ folder git init # Add and commit the changes git add --all git commit -m "first version of the website" # Set origin git remote add origin https://github.com/username/username.github.io.git # Rename local branch git branch -M main # And push your updates online git push -u origin main Voila! 🥳🥳🥳
You got yourself a cool website! Questions? Reference
summary: Setting up a personal website using Blogdown.
title: Hugo, R, and GitHub to build and host personal websites

content:
<!DOCTYPE html> OpenStreetMap with Leaflet IALCCE 2023 It was a great chance to get to know brilliant people working on different aspects of civil engineering. In our special session, we had 6 speakers and had a fruitful discussion after each presentation. The topics presented in the special session include:
Decentralized control-based intervention policies for road networks by Y. Nakazato. Efficient early estimates of bridge interventions: costs, required possession times and associated failure risks by H. Mehranfar. Estimation of bridge component condition states with varying data availability by S. Chuo. State-of-the-art in the use of responsive systems for the built environment by C. Martani. Digital twins in construction practice — A use case driven implementation based on existing theory by I. Zinke. Advanced life-cycle assessment of reinforced concrete bridges using digital twin by J. Rymeš.
summary: Chairing a special session on Exploiting Digitalization in the Intervention Planning for Transportation Infrastructure
title: Presentation at ILACCE 2023

summary: This paper aims to identify and evaluate the bottlenecks and wastes in the loading, unloading, and in-transit processes of iron ore railcars using simulation...
title: Improving operational efficiency of iron ore logistics by rail using simulation

title: Supervising theses and projects

content:
The future of rail transport will be one where trains carrying passengers and goods flow seamlessly from their origins to their destinations. Potential disruptions due to train incidents will be foreseen with the extensive use of surveillance technologies, and either prevented through the implementation of detailed action plans or their consequences eliminated with the seamless rerouting of trains. Interventions on the infrastructure, whether it be the grinding of tracks, or expansion of train stations, will be carried out in perfectly allotted slots in train schedules. Train schedules will be stable and the use of train schedule reserves will be maximized.
STABILITY will develop tools to enable the realization of the latter part of this future. It will include the development of a new integrated train schedule – intervention planning process that enables the optimal exploitation of digital tools. This process will
enable the use of mathematical models built using state‐of‐the‐art concepts, i.e.
mixed integer linear programming models or heuristics to determine the optimal work to be performed at predefined times in train schedules on assets considering asset specific characteristics,
dynamic Bayesian networks to predict the future asset states and their levels of uncertainty, and
multivariate Kriging to ensure correct estimates of current asset states when there are incomplete data sets,
include the automated transfer of asset and intervention specific information contained in multiple databases at specific time points to facilitate the running of the mathematical models to generate idealized windows for interventions, and
include the automated recalculation of the exact activities to ensure train schedule stability between the fixed windows, with increasing levels of exactitude as the windows near. STABILITY will enable a digital revolution in railway intervention planning that will help ensure both train schedule stability and the maximum use of train schedule reserves.
summary: Maintaining train schedule (STABILITY) and managing time table reserves via digitalized railway intervention planning.
title: STABILITY

summary: In this paper, significant factors of freight train collision severity at rail-road level crossing, have been identified using a multinomial logit model...
title: Major Factor Affecting Freight Train Collision Severity At Road-Rail Level Crossing Using Multinomial Logit Model

content: In this project a a digital tool was developed for improving the decision making about the taking different operation policies on how to dispatch and distribute the cargo wagon across the railway network in Iran. The project employed a mixture of optimization models and data analysis to provide optimal solutions for various scenarios for minimizing operational costs, maximizing availability of wagons. The project delivered a user-friendly interface using AnyLogic© that helps the decision makers in planning and executing the fleet allocation strategies.
summary: Facilitating the decision making for allocation of open top cargo wagons across the railway network aiming at improving the availability and profitability.
title: Smart Fleet Allocation

summary: The purpose of this study is to investigate and identify the waste from the unloading station of mineral cars using the lean thinking approach. In this paper, one of the largest steel producers is investigated as a case study...
title: Improving Efficiency of Mineral Terminal Performance using Lean Railroading

content:
Create slides in Markdown with Wowchemy Wowchemy | Documentation
Features Efficiently write slides in Markdown 3-in-1: Create, Present, and Publish your slides Supports speaker notes Mobile friendly slides Controls Next: Right Arrow or Space Previous: Left Arrow Start: Home Finish: End Overview: Esc Speaker notes: S Fullscreen: F Zoom: Alt + Click PDF Export Code Highlighting Inline code: variable
Code block:
porridge = "blueberry" if porridge == "blueberry": print("Eating...") Math In-line math: $x + y = z$
Block math:
$$ f\left( x \right) = ;\frac{{2\left( {x + 4} \right)\left( {x - 4} \right)}}{{\left( {x + 4} \right)\left( {x + 1} \right)}} $$
Fragments Make content appear incrementally
{{% fragment %}} One {{% /fragment %}} {{% fragment %}} **Two** {{% /fragment %}} {{% fragment %}} Three {{% /fragment %}} Press Space to play!
One Two Three A fragment can accept two optional parameters:
class: use a custom style (requires definition in custom CSS) weight: sets the order in which a fragment appears Speaker Notes Add speaker notes to your presentation
{{% speaker_note %}} - Only the speaker can read these notes - Press `S` key to view {{% /speaker_note %}} Press the S key to view the speaker notes!
Only the speaker can read these notes Press S key to view Themes black: Black background, white text, blue links (default) white: White background, black text, blue links league: Gray background, white text, blue links beige: Beige background, dark text, brown links sky: Blue background, thin dark text, blue links night: Black background, thick white text, orange links serif: Cappuccino background, gray text, brown links simple: White background, black text, blue links solarized: Cream-colored background, dark green text, blue links Custom Slide Customize the slide style and background
{{< slide background-image="/media/boards.jpg" >}} {{< slide background-color="#0000FF" >}} {{< slide class="my-style" >}} Custom CSS Example Let’s make headers navy colored.
Create assets/css/reveal_custom.css with:
.reveal section h1, .reveal section h2, .reveal section h3 { color: navy; } Questions? Ask
Documentation

content: In this project a a decision aiding tool was developed for improving the decision making about the taking different operation policies on how to improve the efficiency of operations related to logistics of mineral cargo with rail. The project analyses the current situation and assessed multiple improvement policies using discrete event simulation in Planimate©.
summary: Providing a decision aiding tool for evaluating different operation policies to improve the efficiency of heavy haul transportation
title: Cargo for Rail
